variable_identity.standardized_name_en: Subject to hospital admission identifier map

immutable_core.semantic_definition: A hospital-admission-level identifier map linking patient subject_id to hospital admission hadm_id with admission start and discharge timestamps retained for encounter-window joins.
immutable_core.semantic_grain: one row per hospital admission identifier
immutable_core.same_name_cross_database_rule: Any database asset published as std_id_map_subject_hadm must preserve a patient-to-hospital-admission identifier relationship and admission time window; it must not silently change into ICU-stay mapping, readmission outcome, diagnosis event, demographic category, or follow-up phenotype.

admin_demographic_contract.identifier_scope_rule: retain one row per hospital admission identifier, with subject_id allowed to repeat across admissions
admin_demographic_contract.source_authority_rule: use the database-authoritative hospital admissions table for the mapping; do not infer admission windows from ICU stays or event tables
admin_demographic_contract.normalization_rule: retain source subject_id, hadm_id, admittime, and dischtime after type normalization and uniqueness checks
admin_demographic_contract.no_row_interpretation: no retained row means no approved hospital-admission identifier relationship under the source rule, not absence of hospitalization

execution_governance.formal_output_rule: Formal std_id_map_subject_hadm outputs should go through execution.py reading this variable_spec and an approved mapping_spec.
